reviews.review_text: I would absolutely love to see Magnificus and Gimora's world fully fledged out. Leshy and P03 both had their whole world in the game, but Id die to have the other scrybes worlds. Maybe as DLCs or after you beat the game you can go into their version of the game. Idk either way I love this game to death, I will get all of the achievements one day !
Made with love, one of the best games i have ever played. Theres a stupid amount of detail

I am only writing this comment because I know it won't hurt the developer at this point. The ending completely ruined the game for me. It constantly felt like this game has multiple endings and I thought I had overlooked something. There is so much hinting at the possibility that you can change the outcome or certain other things (like have a secret act with the grave scrybe) of the game. But there isn't. After my first run I looked up what I had done wrong just to find out that this game is completely linear, which makes act 2 and act 3 of the game completely irrelevant for me because they don't give you any kind of challenge. The first act it the only one that really sets back your progress, act 2 dying doesn't even have any  consequences and you can just try over and over again in act 3 with just a minor setback.
I played 25 hours, most of them bored, for one of the most unsatisfying endings I ever experienced. Although I have to say I teared up when seeing Leshy again and him just trying to play another round, even if we can't keep score anymore. Flipping a serial killer into someone I have symapthies for is an amazing job, but it was not enough the make the ending satisfying for me.  I don't feel like I had any agency in the ending and the card games were not challenging enough to make it feel like a victory.
So I am leaving this game with the feeling that I missed something. But there is nothing there anymore... unsatisfying...

Otherwise I have to say this game is an absolute piece of art. Which is why I would not in any way have given this comment if there was any risk of hurting the developer or the game with it.

Played what I thought was a really well designed, atmospheric, strategic turn-based game, only to realise that it was ONLY THE FIRST CHAPTER!
If you've liked any cardgame ever then it's a must try.

The game forces you to mess with 2 of the 4 decks, and I used the 2nd Act to try out Grimora's deck as well. Magnificus' deck is the least realized of the 4 lacking a comparable amount of strategy variety - and the only reason for this is that his deck needs to be experienced (and it's heavily implied) in a first-person keller synth dark fantasy Les Legions Noires raw xeroxed cover tape black metal role-playing FMV dungeon crawler gameplay setting and this comment is an open call to KGB to continue Moloch worship blood sacrifices so that Kola superdeep borehole may emanate this hypothetical game on a VHS tape some day as well.

[spoiler]Technical details: Proton Experimental, the Archivist failed to delete my precious 14 year old Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official video).png screenshot and left a cheesy .txt file alongside it instead.[/spoiler]
[spoiler]Edit: grammar.[/spoiler]

Don't know how I haven't made a review about the game yet but this truly is a masterclass title replaying it after a couple years and it still holds up even after my 2nd playthrough I still have stuff in the game to do.
IF YOU ARE CONSIDERING BUYING INSCRIPTION PURCHASE IT, IT IS ONE OF THE BEST GAMES I HAVE EVER PLAYED

for the first time i play this game, i was mesmerized by the gameplay and enjoying it , but when it came to act 2 and three i was mindblown, like the dev can have such a way to introduce old inscriyption type of game, and with lots of hidden things accross the game it was genuienly briliant game. that's all i can say about this game

If u want a deckbuilder with an actual narrative this is definitely the game for you. It's not too difficult and not too easy that you dont have to think. Gameplay is fun although not as addictive as other deckbuilders but it compensates that by having a story.
This game is kind of like Undertale meets Hearthstone meets holy crap I think I need to sit down for a second just buy it--
9/10 noice story, good gameplay, pretty, sad they didnt include much of the other card makers aswell.

A simple rogue-like deckbuilder that builds into something compelling through atmosphere, a cryptic meta-narrative, and game-changing plot twists. Act 1 slowly drew me in, building intrigue and tension. After Act 1, I felt like the game lost some steam. The mechanics mainly stay the same regarding the card-playing portions of the game, but it has lost the charm of the first act and the extra game mechanics introduced become tedious. Overall, though, I enjoyed the gameplay loop and found the story emotionally moving by the end.

Reminds me a lot of Yu-Gi-Oh this game really also just surprised me once you beat the final boss you realize the game just started... I wont say more this is a great roguelike. For those of you who know the duel disk part just saying they should make that as a full fledged game.

Every 100 games or so, you strike 1 that just leaves a lasting impression. Inscryption is that 1.

Yes, the mechanics change, and arguably get weaker, but that's the point. This is a great game. Play it, and play it to the end.

[i]Inscryption[/i] might genuinely be one of my favorite games ever. Not only because the game itself is insanely fun, but because it completely dragged me into the world of roguelikes and deckbuilders. It got me into [i]Balatro[/i], which got me into [i]Slay the Spire[/i], which now has me obsessively playing [i]Slay the Spire 2[/i] and finally understanding  [i]The Binding of Isaac[/i] properly. This game seriously opened an entire genre for me.

And honestly, after 300 hours, its still replayable. Finding ridiculous combos never gets old. Huge shoutout to [url=https://www.youtube.com/@Exxo]Exxo[/url], [url=https://www.youtube.com/@GoldenRodent]Golden Rodent[/url] (that are not the same person at all) because watching other people completely break the game became part of the fun for me.

The atmosphere is incredible too. The sounds, the card animations, the lighting, the weird uneasy feeling the game constantly gives you — everything works together so perfectly. Few games manage to feel this stylish and unsettling at the same time.

I just wish the game got another huge update or a DLC or something. It’s still alive.
So much fun! and it is super exciting to try and find all of the secrets

Has an exceptionally strong first act, almost too good, since the later parts don’t quite reach the same level. Still, like Daniel Mullins’ previous games, it delivers plenty of twists, turns, and surprises that keep things engaging.  By the end, it even manages to reframe things in ways I didn’t expect, which I appreciated. 4/5 scrybes.

I LOVED the first act of this game, and it was also the only thing I saw of the game before buying it, and when I finished act 1, and act 2 begun, I lost all my interest in it, and as a blessing and a curse, I finished act 1 fast enough that I was able to refund the game. I just wish the game was more act 1, and as much as I'm happy I got to experience and truly enjoy the first act, I knew I was not going to enjoy the rest of the game, even more so after watching videos of it.

I highly recommend this game for it's first act, but there was nothing shown of anything afterwards, you're lead to believe act 1 is the game, and I felt insanely miss-lead when I finished act 1 and there was game I didn't want to play, game I didn't purchase. I have to not recommend this game because if I wasn't able to refund this game, I would have felt incredibly burned. As amazing as act 1 was, it was not worth the amount I paid for it, and if I couldn't refund it, it would spend the rest of my life rotting away in my steam library, never seeing the light of day again.

Please do some research into the later acts of this game before you buy it, if I had done so I wouldn't have bought it in the first place.

Superb
A great deck builder with an amazing amount of depth, especially with [[REDACTED]] and the [[REDACTED]] level. I particularly loved [[REDACTED[[ and [[REDACTED]] [[REDACTED]] [[REDACTED]] [[REDACTED]] Pony. 

Stop reading and just play the game, your a LOT better off not knowing anything.
this game is soooooooooooo good all you need to know is that its a deck builder if you hate it dont play it if you dont, play it its super amazing i cant even say anything. inscryption is peak.
Interesting storytelling and timeline format, fun game mechanics. W indie card game

Act I of the game was really good, but then they decided to make a new game following it in Act II and just when i learned the mechanics of the game, i suddenly need to learn some new mechanics of some new game. Act III tries to remedy this by bringing the player back to the room with a table of cards, but this time again new mechanics and a more boring digital world, instead of the mysterious beast theme of Act I. It tries to tie it all together with some lackluster horror/mystery theme, but without actually thinking about the card mechanics, it feels more like a glue on than any real stakes in the game. During the gameplay it's never explained how the opponent can just slap more and more cards and never care about the blood/bone/energy mechanics and the last few goodbye games in the end have no meaning whatsoever, since you can only win no matter what you choose, you are forced to only click on and listen to the story. It feels like the game was made for the developer to tell their story instead of thinking about the player.

Pains me to leave a bad review, but the game is a bit misleading. The First act of the game is exactly as advertised, retro style graphics, 3d card game mechanics. And so on. Once you complete this act, which is basically the demo. You leave the cabin and restart the game as a 2d Pixel Art card game. While the game plays similar, all of the charm is completely lost and its essentially as if another developer reskinned the game. They do not show photos of this part of the game in the steam page, and because it took me 4 hours to get through act 1, i cannot refund the game. 

The art style and everything in acts 2 and 3 are essentially a poorly designed, ugly pixel art game. You'll have to look up spoilers of the game to see for yourself.
I was expecting a cheap card game, but i found one of the best indie games i've ever played

Just such an incredible time with some really fantastic sequences, narrative and addicting gameplay loops. Second time fully finishing it after 100%ing it on console- just had to give them more money and play again, can't recommend this enough especially if you love deck builders
Honestly I LOVE act 2 so much that if they made it so act 2 is WAY longer, i would be playing it for 12 hrs.... oh and the other ones are fine too.

One of the most Expectation-Shattering, Gold-Standard Class games I've ever had the Pleasure to Rediscover in my Steam Library
10/10 In every sense of the word, An essential must-play to any video-game fan.
Its peak don't even think about it yes its worth it even for full price, if its on sale you need to buy it immediately no excuses.

this was the best game i think ive ever played. the visuals sound and mixed medias were all so amazing and differnt between levels. Not to  mention the coolest storyline ive ever seen in a game. really and truly 10/10
Fun little card game. Make sure to start a new game at least once.
banger game, great story, sick as hell artstyle
Card game is super fun and addicting. Best to go into this game completely blind because it takes some twists and turns that you'll never see coming.

A masterpiece. Fun, strategic, oppressive and a ton of replayability. Never played a game that goes this much outside the box since Undertale and never played a game with such a well crafted ambience since Hollow Knight.
Spooky, layered deck builder/puzzle game. Only three hours in but making progress while learning (and dying)
Just beat the game...what a roller coaster! If you are reading reviews to see if you should buy it or not, BUY IT. This is the best game I have played in a very long time. You won't regret it.

This game is good, there are thousands of reviews that praise it already and I don't know what else I could even add to all that. Really pleased that such graphics can run smoothly on my crappy laptop as well. Also, thank the gods for Ouroboros.
So So Good, you literally are required to play if you use steam, or even a computer.
it really keeps going, like multiple gotcha moments, does some amazing things for "just a card game" WORTH AT FULL PRICE SO DEF AT ANY SALE

Enjoyed the game, I just completed it going to start the next play through.  I really enjoy the core game from the demo, then the game changed bringing nostalgia from a 2000s childhood. I was a bit disappointed it changed then the style got familiar and reminiscent of childhood games of 'creatures in balls' and games of very early days of computers before I was playing them. I highly recommend, this has taken my current favorite over these style games and I've been playing CloverPit for a months. I agree with Steam referral it's similar also to buckshot roulette although much more involved than expected and than buckshot.

Incredible. Simply incredible.

The gameplay loop is so perfectly entertaining - story mode is so enjoyable I often go back to play it, Kaycee's mod makes for a fantastic challenge, or for a nice, mindless grind if the going gets too tough.

I'll say it again, this game is no better described than simply incredible.
I cant think of anything funny or interesting to say here, but the game is good, play the game

Personally, I have been dying to play this game. The concept of this entire game is just mind-bending. It presents itself as a creepy, atmospheric deckbuilder set in a dark cabin, but that’s just the outermost layer of the onion. What makes it so brilliant is how it constantly pulls the rug out from under you. And there is a lot more to this game besides the main story. If you haven't played it yet, do yourself a favor.
I've only just started and I'm already sucked in to this game. Excellent atmosphere, incredible aesthetic, the soundtrack slaps. Play this.

After being introduced to this game by a close friend of mine, I couldn't stop playing it. I enjoyed challenging myself to push decks to limits and try out different combinations instead of just one thing I knew worked. And that's ignoring how great the tone, setting, and lore are.

This is easily my favorite game OAT. I played over a hundred hours on my dad's pc when I was younger, before getting it on my own laptop. It's incredibly well written, and absolutely genius in every way. Finding something new always had my on the edge of my seat in excitement (basically I was almost always half out of the chair)! [spoiler] Despite only having one true ending, it's incredibly re-playable (even without Kacyee's mod). The in-code puzzle was riveting to discover, and every secret sends you down a rabbit hole of excitement. The crossover secrets with the other games also added so much to the experience across all of them (fishing game included), and I can't wait for Pony Island 2: Panda Circus to come out!! [/spoiler] <3333
really engaging and honestly i have been dying for a card like rouge game and this just hit the spot

THE best card game ever created. Love the art, music, spooky vibe, and all the twists. Amazing gameplay and story. Every run is different and there is content after you beat the game with Kaycee's Mod. 10/10 would recommend.
This game was a wild ride but a much needed breath of fresh air. If you like card battle games or story driven games than this game is for you. Just be aware that act 2 is slow...
The first part of the game is great, but the lore feels more annoying than cool.
Hated the switch in the middle of the game. Loved the beginning spooky ambiance but was ruined with the complete switch in game style
its a really well made game and i enjoy playing it since it is easy to follow and interesting, yes i failed a lot but after a break i succeed and continue 10/10 game, i reccomend.
Incredible game. Great lore, very immersive. Fun for any kind of gamer

The game is not perfect. Some parts can feel repetitive, and the story becomes a bit confusing at times. Still, the strong gameplay and memorable experience make it worth playing. Overall: 8/10  A creative and unforgettable game that combines deck-building, horror, and puzzles.

I'm glad went in blind like my friend recommended. I'm usually someone who bounces around from game to game, rarely completing one as I go. When I got this game, I didn't play anything else until I "beat" it. And even then, I'm not really done. If you're intrigued by this game but aren't sure if you should get it, absolutely do. It's unbelievably worth the price, and on sale, I felt like I was stealing. This game is too good to be priced so low, but I'm glad it is more accessible or I wouldn't have been able to play it so soon. Thank you devs for this amazing storytelling experience. For steam deck users I recommend using a capacitive stylus, it was an absolute game changer for comfortability.
Deckbuilding but with a spoopy twist! Refreshing take on card/deck games. Go in blind.

okay so up until the point that the game is what it appears to be it is amazing, but then you realise there are more games within this games and pulling up a wiki every now and then might be useful
whoever's thought of all of this must be pretty cash money
A rare game where the gameplay is so good I want a sequel, but the story is so good I really DON'T want a sequel

Easily one of my favourite games of all time. Such a unique experience. So many unique game elements mashed together and so much lore to discover. I loved every second of this game and I can see all the love and work that went behind this absolute masterpiece.
Very cool card game! This is probably my favorite card game, possibly even favorite video game, EVER! Leshy is a great dungeon master!

Had a blast playing this. There was so much more to the game than I had expected. I knew nothing about it going in. Just when you get used to how the game is played, something comes along to mix it up.

Start of the game: I hate you with every fiber of my being. 
End of the game: You're right, we don't need to keep score.
One of the best game design what i have ever tried. I do like games and this one really blow my mind. This game will break your imagination about the game in general.

Great game. Interesting story. Plenty of puzzles and secrets. I played this for 8 1/2 straight on a livestream on my YT, went to bed, woke up and streamed another 5+ hours. The main game isn't very long but the challenge levels really test your skill (and luck). Extremely replayable. Beat it on PC and Switch. Can't wait for the next project the devs are working on, "Shroom and Gloom".

Finally 100%'d the game and it was SOOOOOOOOOOO worth it. I had taken some time off bc of the final achievement, so much has to go your way and getting a run going can take time, but I finally sat down and did it. I can't recommend this game enough, please play this game. You'll enjoy it

Inscryption is one of the most unique card-based games I've had the pleasure of coming across. The creator, Daniel Mullins, clearly put his heart and soul into creating this beautiful game. Every act is practically an entirely different game, and the fact this game was primarily created by a single developer makes it infinitely more impressive. The storyline is amazing, the gameplay is immersive, and the models are as impressive as they are terrifying, (especially act 1 Leshy.)
For the amount of content and enjoyment this game has brought me, I believe $20USD is a very fair price. If you're able to, I think this game is well worth your time and money!
10/10

It's a pretty addictive game, sometimes it feels really unfair, but when you get there, man, what a feeling. 

It's a fun roguelite deck builder, and it has so many mechanics that you'll find many ways of beating the combats, and also so many that you may unconsciously ruined a hand. The story is really interesting, full of good inmersion mechanics. I recommend it.

Enjoyed the beginning a lot more than what came after it, but overall a really nice game.
Also the lore is insane of this game, the OLD_DATA is some pretty serious stuff.
I really enjoyed the game. It was insane to play and highly suggest it!

Deck building oyunlarında güzel bir oyun. Hem hikaye modu hemde hikaye modu bittikten sonra çıkan rouge like tarzı modu ile beraber çok zevkli bir oyun. Oyunla ilgili tek sıkıntı belirli bir süreden sonra belirli başlı build başlangıçlarının daha op olduğunu ve bunlarla bazı modların tamamlanabileceği haricinde sıkıntısız, akan eğlenceli biraz gizemli güzel bir oyun.
Great Game. Wish it had longer content for Grimora and Magnificus

Card games with story? much fun for me and my partner, we both play it through steam family library and its been fun to talk about. If you enjoy games such as yugioh or magic, you might get a bit of a kick from this.
Got into this from a markiplier video, i love the strategy and card game part, the puzzle part is cool as well.
this is one of the best story game i have played. the replayability is amazing and the story is great.

Just played this game for 4 hours today. Very impressive as you keep finding new things that change the game entirely. Even the way it is played. Definitely one of the most impressive roguelike deck-builders out there. Get it at a discount I would say if you like deck building and weird games that require some exploration.
The card game of all time. In fact, this is the second time I've bought it (first was my Switch). I want to kiss Grimora.

A brilliant 9/10.
The first section of the game is easily the most iconic; a roguelike horror escape room deckbuilding game is a lot of things to be, but it pulls it off effortlessly.
The only point I knock off is for later sections of the game, which never quite acheive the astounding level polish and  pinache of the first act. The good thing is you can replay the first section as a standalone mode... But in this alternative play mode some of the most interesting game-breaking combinations were "fixed." There's also no form of "endless run" where you can continue to play and hone a deck until it breaks under the weight of an overwhelming opponent. I would have loved to see more game-breaking mechanics be preserved and introduced to see how far one can truely push a deck build in an endless run.
But really the main "flaw" is that you can't get enough of the game. It's an incredible value.

I've never enjoyed deck builder / card games.  I recently set up GameNative on my phone and wanted something I could play offline with touch input while on an airplane and figured I'd give it a try.  This is not your typical card game and is awesome.  There's still a lot about the mechanics I haven't caught, but the storytelling and other puzzles in this game keep it from being "draw card, play card" over and over and over.  I'm 3 hours in and had to force myself to stop to go to bed.  Also it plays perfectly on my Android phone.
Best card builder horror game there is; first playthrough is incredible in all aspects

not a fan of both card games and rogue likes, and yet this game made me crave for more after completing it twice. Thank god for the enhanced roguelike mode after completing the game

Go try it out, it doesn't cost that much
First time playing a deckbuilder game, cozy, spooky, and unexpected! Love it so far :)
Amazing card game, captivating story

HIGHLY RECOMMEND
I've purchased this game on PlayStation and PC and have intentions to purchase another copy for the family library.
If you enjoy kitschy games, I encourage you to check out any and all of Daniel Mullin's works. If you enjoy a slightly intense threatening vibe, then start with INSCRYPTION!
INSCRYPTION is a meta-game that blends in real-world superstition. You will play for the Main Character, but you will not BE the main character outside of playing the game. It was fun and weird and is perfect for people who enjoy psychological games.
My main hope is that someday we see a continuation to learn more about the code.

I rarely seen a game that continuesly evolves its gameplay so interestingly throughout its entire runtime. absolutely top10 card games of all time. Oh and the world building and story telling evolves just as freakily. amazing 9/10
It's a really cool game and the design rocks, but I can't seem to get past the Angler and the challenge stopped being fun a handful of runs ago.
这可能是我玩过最惊艳我的卡牌游戏了，解密和规则都很特别。用相机照莱西的时候我就知道这个游戏不简单。在之后还有虚拟转现实的情节

Forget the trailer, the marketing, all of it. I wholeheartedly recommend this game to anyone who loves video games, regardless of your budget. This is one of the highest forms the medium can reach, yet it’s probably the kind of masterpiece that will quietly slip past most people unnoticed.

This isn’t just a game. It feels like a manifestation of the endless possibilities that the human mind, yours, mine, everyone’s, is capable of creating. So please, play it, and if you can, make others play it too.

I don’t know… maybe I’m just tired of constantly seeing games labeled as “depression killers” or “the next Dark Souls” on YouTube, as if people are trying to redirect much deeper feelings into buzzwords and trends. But this game genuinely hit me in a different way. As someone in their late thirties dealing with existential absurdities and the strange weight of being alive, this experience shook me awake with a strangely brutal honesty.

And I truly hope it can give even a small spark of that feeling to someone else too.

Good luck, and enjoy the game.
This game was collecting dust like so many other games in my library, and after playing it I realized my dumbass should have played this much sooner. Just absolute cinema, can only recommend it.

God, I love Inscryption. Fun mechanics, amazing story, and beautiful presentation. 

Without any spoilers, this game is a rollercoaster ride that starts out slow but has an amazing finish. I can definitely see how some players may not be a huge fan of the twists and turns Inscryption takes, but as a fan of older deck builders like the Pokemon TCG for the gameboy, and game development as an art form, this game was a solid treat for me. 

Highly recommend for anyone looking for a new deck builder roguelite.

Non-spoiler part:
Love this game, very fun and original and engaging.

Spoiler ahead - additional thoughts:
[spoiler]
I LOVED Act 1. Please make more and full games along these lines. So fun to have Leshy be the GM for this adventure.  Act 2 was fun in its own right, but too different and could've been a different game entirely. Didn't like Act 3, felt like a slog. [/spoiler]

This game is a blast. Its deceving about its gameplay and the entire game changes 3 different times but its so much fun every time. The story is pretty interesting with a good ending that takes over your pc a little bit. The gameplay is where this game shines though because the card games are super clever and easy to learn. For example there's moments where your friends appear as cards with random stats or there's this other time where you use internet files to determine your points so you don't lose. The game is pretty deciving on how long the game actually is considering you think the game is finished when you are only a third of the way in. Id say the first part is the most interesting and the most fun ( wooden tree guy ) considering there's more in that like the woodcarver and the best boss fights. speaking of boss fights its pretty funny how many of them are in this game and its especially funny at the end where they try to fit one more boss fight in. Besides that id rate this game a 8 or 9 out of 10
rly great story, fascinating gameplay, and awesome replayability especially because of kaycees mod
fun and interesting game, challenging.
Very Very Very cool game, very fun very intriguing, very swag
amazing game. awesome creepy vibes, super fun story, I love the escape room style of Act 1 & 2, genuinely one of the best games I've ever played

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.
I said the above phrase at least 10 times during this game. There are so many twists, and it has, in my opinion, one of the best stories in gaming. And the ending is INSANE, Tip, though: there's an ARG you can do that you access from the game; just look up the results. Don't try to do it solo, it's pretty much impossible to do that. Final note: the theme for the Trapper boss fight is the best boss theme I have ever heard. Like, I listen to it outside of the game.
as an yu gi oh fan who didnt play after the cards had to much bs as effect ts feels really nice
Love the game and the replayability, i originali saw it from a markiplayer video thought it looked fun so i gave it a shot and my god... It slaps hard! 9/10 paws
definitely one of the best card horror games ive played. I love the storyline and the lore behind it
Great concept. Story is good and gameplay is addictingly fun.
One of the coolest games I have ever played! Felt very uneasy the entire time. Like a cross between Undertale and Buckshot Roulette, of all things
Loved it until i beat the game, then turns wierd, but still fun.
